docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
PlexAniBridge is a tool designed to keep your AniList profile automatically synced with your Plex activity. Whether you're tracking your watch progress, ratings, or reviews, PlexAniBridge has you covered.

Quick Start Docker | Quick Start Source | Configuration

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Comprehensive Synchronization: Tracks watch status, progress, repeat counts, ratings, text reviews, and start/end dates.
  • Smart Content Mapping: Matches Plex content (movies, shows, specials, etc.) to AniList using a robust mappings database with fuzzy title search as a fallback and the ability to add custom media mappings.
  • Efficient Scanning: Supports various modes of scanning like partial, full, or polling scans to minimize API usage.
  • Flexible Scheduling: Configurable synchronization intervals in addition to polling capabilities.
  • Multi-User Support: Sync multiple Plex users and home users with their respective AniList users.
  • Optimized Performance: Intelligent caching of requests to minimize API rate limits.
  • Easy Deployment: Fully compatible with Docker deployments 🐳.

docs.title: Acknowledgments
docs.text:
• Kometa Mappings: The inspiration behind the mappings database.
  • Python-PlexAPI: Powerful and straightforward Plex API bindings.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Below is an example .env file for PlexAniBridge with example values. Optional environment variables are commented out.

.env
#= AniList =#
ANILIST_TOKEN=eyJ...

#= Plex =#
PLEX_TOKEN=2Sb...
PLEX_USER=username
PLEX_URL=http://localhost:32400
PLEX_SECTIONS=["Anime", "Anime Movies"]
# PLEX_GENRES=[]

#= General =#
# SYNC_INTERVAL=3600
# POLLING_SCAN=False
# FULL_SCAN=False
# DESTRUCTIVE_SYNC=False
# EXCLUDED_SYNC_FIELDS=["notes", "score"]

#= Advanced =#
# DATA_PATH=./data
# LOG_LEVEL=INFO
# DRY_RUN=False
# FUZZY_SEARCH_THRESHOLD=90

docs.title: ANILIST_TOKEN
docs.text:
str | list[str] (Required)

AniList API access token.

Generate AniList Token

Multiple Users
  • Provide a list of tokens: ["token1", "token2", "token3"]
  • Each token must have a corresponding Plex user in PLEX_USER.

docs.title: PLEX_TOKEN
docs.text:
str (Required)

Plex API access token.

Finding the Plex Token

Note

The token must belong to the admin user of the Plex server.

docs.title: PLEX_USER
docs.text:
str | list[str] (Required)

Plex user(s) to sync. Can be identified by:

  • Plex account username: "username"
  • Plex account email: "user@email.com"
  • Plex Home user name: "Home User"
Multiple Users
  • Provide a list of users: ["user1", "user2@email.com", "Home User"]
  • Each user must have a corresponding AniList token in ANILIST_TOKEN.
Multi-User Limitations

Due to limitations in the Plex API, only the admin user can sync reviews and watch lists. All other features are available for all users.

docs.title: PLEX_URL
docs.text:
str (Required)

URL to your Plex server that the PlexAniBridge host can access.

Docker Networking

If both Plex and PlexAniBridge are running as Docker containers in the same network, you can use the service name as the URL: http://plex:32400

docs.title: PLEX_SECTIONS
docs.text:
list[str] (Required)

List of Plex library sections to consider, specified in Python list syntax:

["Anime", "Anime Movies"]
Multi-User Considerations

Every section will be synced for every user. If a user doesn't have access to a section, it will be skipped.

docs.title: PLEX_GENRES
docs.text:
list[str] (Optional, default: [])

An optional list of Plex genres to filter by. If specified, only items with these genres will be synced.

["Anime", "Animation"]

This is useful for syncing only Anime content in a mixed library.

Allowing All Genres

To sync all genres, set this to an empty list: [] or don't set it at all.

Finding Possible Genres

Genres are sources from the metdata you use (typically TheMovieDB or TheTVDB). You can find the possible genres below:

  • TheTVDB Genres
  • TheMovieDB Genres

docs.title: SYNC_INTERVAL
docs.text:
int (Optional, default: 3600)

Interval in seconds between sync jobs. Set to -1 to run once and exit.

Sync Interval with Polling Scan

If POLLING_SCAN is enabled, the sync interval determines how often the mappings database and your AniList profile are updated. Periodic scans will be disabled.

docs.title: POLLING_SCAN
docs.text:
bool (Optional, default: False)

When enabled, PlexAniBridge will detect changes in your Plex library in real-time instead of waiting for the sync interval.

Sync Interval with Polling Scan

If enabled, the sync interval determines how often the mappings database and your AniList profile are updated.

docs.title: FULL_SCAN
docs.text:
bool (Optional, default: False)

Scans all Plex media, regardless of activity. By default, only watched items are scanned.

Note

Full scans are generally not recommended unless used with DESTRUCTIVE_SYNC.

Warning

Enabling FULL_SCAN can lead to excessive API usage and longer processing times.

docs.title: DESTRUCTIVE_SYNC
docs.text:
bool (Optional, default: False)

Allows regressive updates and deletions, which can cause data loss.

Warning

Enable only if you understand the implications.

Destructive sync allows:

  • Deleting AniList entries.
  • Making regressive updates (e.g., if AniList progress is higher than Plex, AniList will be lowered to match Plex).

Note

To delete AniList entries for unwatched Plex content, enable both FULL_SCAN and DESTRUCTIVE_SYNC.

docs.title: EXCLUDED_SYNC_FIELDS
docs.text:
list[str] (Optional, default: ["notes", "score"])

Specifies which fields should not be synced. Available fields:

  • status (planning, current, completed, dropped, paused)
  • score (rating on a 0-10 scale)
  • progress (episodes watched)
  • repeat (rewatch count)
  • notes (text reviews)
  • started_at (start date)
  • completed_at (completion date)

Allowing All Fields

To sync all fields, set this to an empty list: [] or don't set it at all.

docs.title: DATA_PATH
docs.text:
str (Optional, default: ./data)

Path to store the database, backups, and custom mappings.

Docker

If running in Docker, do not change this path unless properly mapped in your Docker volume/mount.

docs.title: LOG_LEVEL
docs.text:
str (Optional, default: INFO)

Sets logging verbosity. Available levels:

  • DEBUG
  • INFO
  • SUCCESS
  • WARNING
  • ERROR
  • CRITICAL

Minimal Logging

For minimal logging, set the verbosity to SUCCESS which only logs successful operations like syncing entries.

Debugging

For the most detailed logs, set this to DEBUG.

docs.title: DRY_RUN
docs.text:
bool (Optional, default: False)

When enabled:

  • AniList data is not modified.
  • Logs show what changes would have been made.

First Run

Run with DRY_RUN enabled on first launch to preview changes without modifying your AniList data.

docs.title: FUZZY_SEARCH_THRESHOLD
docs.text:
int (Optional, default: 90)

Determines the similarity percentage required for AniList title matching.

Title matching is only used when a mapping could not be found in your custom mappings or the mapping database.

  • Lower values allow more matches (but may increase false positives).
  • Higher values allow fewer matches (but reduce false positives).
Disabling Fuzzy Search

Set this to 100 to only allow exact matches.

Disabling Search

Set this to -1 to diable search fallback entirely. This makes it so only mappings in the database or custom mappings are used.

docs.title: Custom Mappings
docs.text: PlexAniBridge allows you to define custom mappings for Plex content to AniList, complementing the default mappings database. This feature is particularly helpful for content that is missing or incorrectly mapped in the default database.

docs.title: Local Custom Mappings
docs.text:
Custom mappings are stored in a JSON file named mappings.custom.json, located in the DATA_PATH directory. Any mappings added here will take precedence over existing entries in the default database.

Note

Entries in the custom mappings file merge with the pre-existing entries, they do not override them. This means that if you add a custom mapping for a series that is already in the default database, only the fields specified in the custom mapping will be updated. The remaining pre-existing fields will remain unchanged.

The custom mappings file adheres to the same JSON schema used in PlexAniBridge-Mappings. You can refer to the default database or the schema for guidance. Below is an example of a mappings.custom.json file:

mappings.custom.json
{
  "$schema": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/eliasbenb/PlexAniBridge-Mappings/v2/mappings.schema.json",
  "1006": {
    "tvdb_mappings": {
      "s0": "e2"
    }
  },
  "1060": {
    "anidb_id": 150,
    "imdb_id": "tt0366064",
    "mal_id": 1060,
    "tmdb_show_id": 19238,
    "tvdb_id": 73492,
    "tvdb_mappings": {
      "s1": ""
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Remote Custom Mappings
docs.text:
If you want to contribute your custom mappings to the community, you can submit a pull request to the PlexAniBridge-Mappings repository. Your pull request should modify the mappings.edits.json and not the mappings.json file.

To browse the existing mappings with a web UI, you can use the PlexAniBridge-Mappings Query Builder.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Docker and Docker Compose
  • AMD64 or ARM64 CPU architecture (or build the image yourself for other architectures)

docs.title: Docker Compose
docs.text:
Below is a PlexAniBridge Docker compose file with example values. Optional environment variables are commented out.

Tip

Have a look at the configuration page for a detailed list of configurable environment variables.

compose.yaml
services:
  plexanibridge:
    image: ghcr.io/eliasbenb/plexanibridge:latest
    environment:
      ANILIST_TOKEN: eyJ...
      PLEX_TOKEN: 2Sb...
      PLEX_USER: username
      PLEX_URL: http://plex:32400
      PLEX_SECTIONS: '["Anime", "Anime Movies"]'
      # PLEX_GENRES: []
      # SYNC_INTERVAL: 3600
      # POLLING_SCAN: False
      # FULL_SCAN: False
      # DESTRUCTIVE_SYNC: False
      # EXCLUDED_SYNC_FIELDS: '["notes", "score"]'
      # DATA_PATH: ./data
      # DRY_RUN: False
      # LOG_LEVEL: INFO
      # FUZZY_SEARCH_THRESHOLD: 90
    volumes:
      - ./data:/app/data
      # - ./logs:/app/logs
    restart: unless-stopped

To start the container, run:

docker compose -f compose.yaml up -d

Tip

You can pin the image to a specific version or branch by changing latest to a specific tag. Some available tags are:

  • latest: The latest stable release
  • beta: The latest beta release (may be unstable)
  • alpha: The latest alpha release (may be unstable)
  • vX.Y.Z: A specific version from the releases page (e.g. v0.4.0)
  • main: The latest commit on the main branch, which is usually tied to the latest release
  • develop: The latest commit on the develop branch (may be unstable)

docs.title: Docker CLI
docs.text:
Below is a minimal example of a Docker run command with only the required variables.

docker run \
  --name plexanibridge \
  -e ANILIST_TOKEN \
  -e PLEX_TOKEN \
  -e PLEX_USER \
  -e PLEX_URL \
  -e PLEX_SECTIONS \
  -v ./data:/app/data \
  ghcr.io/eliasbenb/plexanibridge:latest

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • SQLite3
  • Git

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Tip

Have a look at the configuration page for a detailed list of configurable environment variables.

git clone https://github.com/eliasbenb/PlexAniBridge.git
cd PlexAniBridge

pip install -r requirements.txt

cp .env.example .env # Edit the .env file

python main.py

docs.title: Disaster Recovery
docs.text:
Given that software will always be susceptible to bugs, PlexAniBridge offers a backup and restore feature to help you recover from any data loss.

Prevention

Before running PlexAniBridge for the first time, it is recommended to try a dry run to see what changes will be made without actually making them. This can help you identify any potential issues before they occur.

docs.title: Backups
docs.text:
Before any scheduled sync job, PlexAniBridge will create a backup of your current AniList data. These backups are stored under the data folder (defined in DATA_PATH) in the backups directory as JSON files.

Warning

These backups are automatically deleted after 7 days.

To restore from a backup, use the restore script in the scripts folder. You will need to pass the backup file and AniList token as arguments:

pip install requests pydantic # Python 3.10+
python scripts/anilist_restore.py --token <YOUR_ANILIST_TOKEN> ./data/backups/plexanibridge-user.1.json
